summary: This is a very brief follow-up post noting that Dell fixed their website search engine after Joel's earlier complaint, while Starbucks still hasn't addressed his gripe about their use of grease pencils for marking cups. Joel uses the contrast to highlight the difference between companies that respond to usability criticism and those that don't.
key_points: Dell responded to public criticism by fixing their search engine within weeks
Starbucks continued using grease pencils despite Joel's earlier complaint about the practice
Public criticism from influential voices can sometimes drive rapid change in tech companies
money_quotes: So why is Starbucks still using their damned grease pencils?
key_insight: Tech companies may respond quickly to public usability complaints, but entrenched offline processes are far harder to change.
